titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the James Rorty Papers
titlestmt.author: by Alison Clemens

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 19, 2018 at 04:44

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Manuscripts, printed works, correspondence, and professional papers of the writer James Rorty. The Writings series includes Rorty's typescript and manuscript essays on social issues, civil rights, and political activism, two of his poems, and printed works. The Correspondence series includes three typed letters between Rorty's wife Winifred Rorty and Matthew Josephson; letters between Rorty and Jerré E. Tanner regarding Tanner's setting of Rorty's poem "A Spring Garland" for voice and piano, with autograph manuscript score; and one typed letter from Rorty to Ruth Aley and one typed letter from Rorty to Tom Sloane. The Professional Papers include Rorty's curriculum vitae.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The James Rorty Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: James Rorty Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization in 2014. Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.
This collection includes materials previously identified by multiple call numbers: Uncat Za MS Rorty and Uncat Za File.

bioghist.head: James Rorty (1890-1973)
bioghist.p: James Rorty (1890-1973), writer, journalist, poet, and father of philosopher Richard Rorty.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscripts, printed works, correspondence, and professional papers of the writer James Rorty. The Writings series includes Rorty's typescript and manuscript essays on social issues, civil rights, and political activism, two of his poems, and printed works. The Correspondence series includes three typed letters between Rorty's wife Winifred Rorty and Matthew Josephson; letters between Rorty and Jerré E. Tanner regarding Tanner's setting of Rorty's poem "A Spring Garland" to music, as well as the original sheet music; and one typed letter from Rorty to Ruth Aley and one typed letter from Rorty to Tom Sloane. The Professional Papers include Rorty's curriculum vitae.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Writings, 1941-1965. II. Correspondence, 1965-1969. III. Professional Papers, 1970, undated.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into two subseries: Manuscripts and Printed Material.

did.unittitle: It Happened Here , manuscript and typescript
The End of the Line , typescript
We Open the Gates: Labor's Fight for Equality , typescript
At the Court of Love , typescript
The Gift of Henry Vann , typescripts
The Wound and the Arrow , typescripts
The Metaphysics of Birth Control , typescript
Roadway to the Future , typescript, corrected
Time and Western Man , typescripts, corrected
TVA and the Common Mooring , typescript fragments, corrected
The Epoch of Man's Becoming , typescript, corrected
The South Jamaica Story , typescript, corrected
A Novel about Anti-Gentilism , typescript book review, corrected
Unidentified essay on advertising, typescript fragment, corrected

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript poem, with typescript essay on ecology

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Rorty's article ¿Hay Descriminación en México?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two issues containing Rorty's Desegregation Along the Mason-Dixon Line .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three issues, containing the Rorty writings A Study in Cultural Elephantiasis (12 May 1944); Did They Know It Was Loaded (3 June 1949); and Independence, Like It or Not (8 September 1961).

did.unittitle: How St. Louis Broke the Race Barrier by Rorty
March of the Machine by Rorty

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three issues, containing the Rorty writings VOGT: Soil and the Future , reviewed by Rorty (11 September 1948); John T. Flynn on the China Story (14 January 1952); a letter to the editor (28 January 1952); and Is McCarthy Subversive (16 August 1954);

title.part: Toward the Integration of Our Schools: Final Report of the Commission on Integration

did.unittitle: Unfitting the Unborn by Rorty and N. Philip Norman
We Open the Gates: Labor's Fight for Equality by Rorty and Harry Fleischman

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is arranged alphabetically by correspondent.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters between Tanner and Rorty regarding the creation of sheet music by Tanner based on Rorty's poem cycle A Spring Garland , as well as the original sheet music.
